title: Would it be possible to kindly include the buildah binary in the Releases?
body: Would it be possible to kindly include the buildah binary in the Releases?

comments.body:
If you mean a fully static binary for linux, I imagine this could be doable since we're doing it already for podman.

@flouthoc @ashley-cui would be nice to have this whenever we're updating the release process for buildah.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Hi all,

Any updates on this?
No update yet, but meanwhile, could you make do with the [buildah container image](https://quay.io/repository/buildah/stable) ?

Hi @lsm5 

Unfortunately no, I was looking for the binary to run it in a CI/CD pipeline and podman is not available either.

While the container option is promising, the most viable solution I can think about in the environment I'm working in is, if I can host the container as an API service and call it from my CI/CD pipeline.

I'm looking for only two things, build an image and push that image to a container registry.

If you have any solution that can work like that, that would be awesome.

If you want more information about my environment, I can provide that too.

@shankar-devops-18 Perhaps vendor Buildah into your own project and call things from there?  I don't know if that's feasable or not, but I know this project does some of what you're looking for: https://github.com/openshift/builder

As far as us creating a Buildah image, it's possible, but the folks tagged by @lsm5 have other commits through the end of this year and it's not likely we'll get to it prior to then.

Buildah is not a standalone staticly linked application, it requires other tools like oci runtimes (crun, runc) as well as potentially container-selinux 

 $ rpm -q --requires buildah
containers-common-extra
...
$ rpm -q --requires containers-common-extra
(crun if fedora-release-identity-server)
(qemu-user-static-aarch64 if fedora-release-identity-server)
(qemu-user-static-arm if fedora-release-identity-server)
(qemu-user-static-x86 if fedora-release-identity-server)
container-network-stack
containers-common = 5:0.64.2-1.fc43
netavark >= 2:1.10.3-1
oci-runtime
passt
rpmlib(CompressedFileNames) <= 3.0.4-1
rpmlib(FileDigests) <= 4.6.0-1
rpmlib(PayloadFilesHavePrefix) <= 4.0-1
rpmlib(PayloadIsZstd) <= 5.4.18-1
rpmlib(RichDependencies) <= 4.12.0-1
rpmlib(TildeInVersions) <= 4.10.0-1

This means Buildah needs to be installed as a package. and pull in all of its requirements.
